tells: The 'metal foam' front reads as flat procedural noise — pores have no real depth, self-shadowing, or connected cell webbing, and the pattern smears uniformly under blur
Depth-of-field and bloom are applied uniformly across the frame with obvious added green/magenta chromatic fringing on edges, a post-process look rather than lens optics
The ground is a perfectly clean infinite-sweep gradient plane with zero dust, texture, or believable contact-shadow detail
Speaker-grille perforations are mathematically uniform with no machining variance, dust, or moire anywhere — beyond what even studio product photography achieves
The unit floats on a seamless pure-white field with an idealized soft gradient and no contact shadow or sensor noise, consistent with marketing CGI press assets
The specular sweep across the aluminum is perfectly smooth with no anodize grain or micro-blemish at any point
Background bokeh of the server-rack LEDs forms irregular optical discs with natural overlap — genuine shallow-DOF lens behavior
The wood desk shows real grain, scuffs, and dust specks, and shadow regions carry visible sensor noise
The foam front panel has genuinely irregular open-cell pores with varying depth and internal shadowing
The 'foam' sides are sparse spiky displacement noise, not the connected open-cell webbing of real reticulated metal foam
Chamfers and edge highlights are impossibly perfect and completely noise-free across the whole chassis
Heavy synthetic chromatic aberration and vignette are layered uniformly over an otherwise sterile black backdrop
Authentically cluttered environment — sheet music, reading glasses, cables, notepad — with natural, uneven lighting falloff
